skill.name: storage-debug-instrumentation
skill.description: Guides agents through adding backend storage diagnostics for PostgreSQL, ChromaDB, startup timing, drift checks, and a Next.js debug dashboard.
skill.summary: Add storage-layer diagnostics, drift checks, startup metrics, and a debug dashboard.
skill.author: BenderFendor

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution and weak cryptography are false positives caused by markdown inline code and ordinary text in SKILL.md. The meaningful risk is that the skill asks agents to add raw storage inspection endpoints and a debug dashboard without specifying authentication, authorization, or production gating.

medium_findings.title: Debug Endpoints May Expose Raw Storage Data
medium_findings.description: The skill directs creation of endpoints for startup metrics, raw ChromaDB articles, raw PostgreSQL articles, and storage drift samples. It does not require authentication, authorization, environment gating, or redaction, so an implementation could expose internal content and operational metadata if deployed broadly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and dashboard requirements are explicit, and they include raw database and vector-store browsing. The actual repository code is not present here, so the absence of access controls is a guidance risk rather than confirmed exploitable code.

low_findings.title: Localhost Network URLs in Verification Steps
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are localhost verification targets for debug endpoints and a local dashboard. They do not indicate external data exfiltration, but they reinforce that the skill creates network-accessible diagnostic surfaces.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs use localhost and appear only in a verification checklist. This is a false positive for malicious networking, with a minor operational exposure concern if similar routes are published without controls.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unauthenticated Debug Surface Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The instructions add API routes and a dashboard that browse raw storage records and drift data, but they omit required safeguards such as admin-only access, local-only deployment, or redaction.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The diagnostic surface is central to the skill and is clearly described. Risk depends on the target application implementation, so confidence is high for the pattern but not maximal for exploitability.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Inline Code Misclassified as Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The repeated static command-execution alerts are caused by markdown inline code for file paths, function names, and endpoint names. No executable Ruby or shell backtick code is present in SKILL.md.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The only scanned file is markdown, and the flagged backticks delimit documentation text. There is no script body or command substitution context in the evidence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak-cryptography alerts point to documentation text such as the description and filter names, not to cryptographic APIs or hash implementations. No evidence of weak cryptographic code was found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose or checklist items, not code that selects a cryptographic algorithm. The static pattern does not match semantic evidence in this file.

content.value_statement: Storage bugs are hard to diagnose when database, vector store, and startup timing data are scattered. This skill guides agents to add focused diagnostics, drift checks, API bindings, and a dashboard.
actual_capabilities: Defines startup metrics instrumentation for backend initialization phases.
Specifies PostgreSQL pagination helpers for article inspection and filtering.
Specifies ChromaDB listing helpers for stored document inspection.
Adds debug API route requirements for startup, ChromaDB, database, and drift data.
Describes a Next.js dashboard with tabs, filters, pagination, and summary metrics.
Includes verification steps for local endpoints and dashboard behavior.
limitations: Does not provide complete source files or ready-to-run implementation code.
Does not define authentication, authorization, or production access controls.
Assumes a FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL, ChromaDB, and Next.js frontend structure.
Does not include automated tests or schema migration guidance.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Storage Drift
use_cases.description: Compare PostgreSQL article mappings with ChromaDB document IDs to find missing or dangling records.

use_cases.title: Review Startup Performance
use_cases.description: Record backend startup phases so teams can see slow database, cache, vector store, or RSS refresh steps.

use_cases.title: Build Internal Debug Views
use_cases.description: Create a local dashboard for browsing storage state, filters, pagination, and diagnostic summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Storage Diagnostics
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a compatible backend and frontend.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to inspect my backend and propose the exact files and steps needed for storage debug instrumentation.

prompt_templates.title: Add Startup Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement the startup metrics service and wire it into my FastAPI startup sequence. Include safe defaults and clear event names.

prompt_templates.title: Create Drift APIs
prompt_templates.scenario: Add storage inspection and drift comparison endpoints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add the database and ChromaDB helper methods, then create debug API endpoints for article browsing and storage drift detection.

prompt_templates.title: Harden Debug Dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Build the debug dashboard and add admin-only access, local development gating, redaction, and tests for every debug route.

output_examples.input: Add storage diagnostics to a FastAPI and Next.js app.
output_examples.output: A scoped implementation plan covering startup metrics, storage helpers, debug routes, frontend API bindings, and a dashboard.

output_examples.input: Find why articles are missing from vector search.
output_examples.output: A drift report design showing missing ChromaDB entries, dangling vector records, counts, and small review samples.
A startup timeline design with phase durations, notes, metadata, and dashboard summary metrics.

best_practices: Gate all debug routes behind admin authentication or local-only configuration.
Redact sensitive fields before returning raw database or vector-store records.
Keep debug endpoints additive and avoid schema changes unless separately reviewed.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw debug endpoints on public production routes.
Do not return full documents when previews and identifiers are enough.
Do not add sync or repair actions without authorization, audit logging, and rollback plans.

faq.question: What stack does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets FastAPI, PostgreSQL, ChromaDB, and a Next.js frontend.

faq.question: Does it include complete implementation code?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured implementation checklist and expected behavior.

faq.question: Can this be used in production?
faq.answer: Only with strong access controls, redaction, and environment gating for debug routes.

faq.question: What data can the dashboard show?
faq.answer: It can show startup timing, raw article previews, storage filters, and drift counts.

faq.question: Does it change database schemas?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that changes should be additive and avoid schema changes.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill creates useful debug surfaces, but it does not require access controls by default.
